title: Ensure that the --service-account-lookup argument is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --service-account-lookup argument is set to true
input_row.description: Validate service account before validating token.
input_row.rationale: If --service-account-lookup is not enabled, the apiserver only verifies that the authentication token is valid, and does not validate that the service account token mentioned in the request is actually present in etcd. This allows using a service account token even after the corresponding service account is deleted. This is an example of time of check to time of use security issue.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that if the --service-account-lookup argument exists it is set to true .
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the below parameter. --service-account-lookup=true Alternatively, you can delete the --service-account-lookup parameter from this file so that the default takes effect.
input_row.default_value: By default, --service-account-lookup argument is set to true .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_service_account_lookup_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the configuration of the API server.
- The specific command to retrieve the API server flags is:
  ```
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'
  ```
- This command will return the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver container.
- The automation will specifically look for the `--service-account-lookup` flag.
- **Pass Criteria:** The output should include `--service-account-lookup=true`.
- **Fail Criteria:** If the output does not include `--service-account-lookup=true`, or if the flag is absent, it indicates that the setting is not configured as required.
